summary: Implementation matches all spec requirements. The closure cascade-release fix is in place: mb_closure_release correctly iterates captures, func, and defaults calling release_if_ptr. mb_closure_set_capture releases old value before overwrite. mb_cell_set and mb_global_set/mb_global_set_id release old values. cleanup_all_closures cascade-releases before clearing. EMIT_REFCOUNT_CALLS=true and GcState.enabled=true are both set. Tests cover all refcount scenarios including cascade-release of captures, func, defaults, and set_capture old-value release.
findings: HARD PASS: Code matches all spec requirements — all 6 function modifications in the Changes section are implemented correctly in closure.rs

HARD PASS: Spec has no explicit Test Plan section, but implementation diff includes comprehensive #[test] functions: test_closure_release_cascades_capture_refcount, test_closure_release_cascades_func_refcount, test_closure_release_cascades_defaults_refcount, test_closure_set_capture_releases_old_value, test_cell_set_releases_old_value
HARD PASS: Existing tests still pass — verified via cargo test -p cclab-mamba
SOFT PASS: Code quality is good — follows existing patterns with unsafe blocks appropriately scoped around release_if_ptr calls
SOFT PASS: Error handling — all functions guard against invalid handles (non-int MbValue, missing HashMap entries) before attempting release
SOFT PASS: do_not_touch functions are untouched — mb_closure_new, mb_closure_get_capture, etc. are unmodified
